senses.definition: used to imply that people are never completely satisfied and are always seeking more wealth, power, or other forms of success, and that this drive can lead to both positive and negative outcomes

examples.x: Maria's boss was never satisfied with her work, and she realized that desire has no limit, as no matter how much she improved, he always wanted more.

word_list.word: (many|) go (out|) for wool and come home shorn
word_list.pronunciation: mˈɛni ɡˌoʊ ˈaʊt fɔːɹ wˈʊl ænd kˈʌm hˈoʊm ʃˈɔːɹn

senses.definition: used to imply that when someone sets out to gain something, often through dishonest or risky means, they are likely to end up losing everything they have, including what they started with

examples.x: Joe was tempted by a get-rich-quick scheme, but his friend reminded him that go for wool and come home shorn, and advised him to stick to more reliable investments.

senses.definition: used to suggest that when someone is too greedy or selfish, they are less likely to achieve their goals or gain what they want

word_list.word: the more you (get|have), the more you want
word_list.pronunciation: ðə mˈoːɹ juː ɡɛt hˈæv ðə mˈoːɹ juː wˈɑːnt

senses.definition: used to suggest that as someone gains more possessions, wealth, or success, their desires tend to grow, leading to a never-ending cycle of wanting more

word_list.word: pigs get fat, hogs get slaughtered
word_list.pronunciation: pˈɪɡz ɡɛt fˈæt hˈɑːɡz ɡɛt slˈɔːɾɚd

senses.definition: used to emphasize the importance of balance and moderation, and warns that taking more than one needs or is entitled to can be dangerous and lead to negative outcomes or loss

word_list.word: (he|) who grasps at too much loses (everything|it all)
word_list.pronunciation: hiː hˌuː ɡɹˈæsps æt tˈuː mʌtʃ lˈuːzᵻz ˈɛvɹɪθˌɪŋ ɪt ˈɔːl

senses.definition: used to caution against being overly ambitious, as trying to take on too much or being too greedy can lead to failure or loss

examples.x: Mark was eager to invest all his savings into a new business opportunity, but his financial advisor cautioned him that who grasps at too much loses everything, and recommended a more conservative approach.

senses.definition: used to suggest that attempting to have or do everything at once can lead to negative consequences and that people should focus on what is essential and achievable

word_list.word: a person with too much ambition cannot sleep in peace
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ pˈɜːsən wɪð tˈuː mʌtʃ æmbˈɪʃən kænˈɑːt slˈiːp ɪn pˈiːs

senses.definition: used to suggest that when someone is overly ambitious, they may experience anxiety or restlessness, as they are constantly striving for more and worrying about achieving their goals
